prose:
**Furan frontier-orbital analysis** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, PySCF; density fitting on by default), **gas phase** (no solvent model), **charge 0, multiplicity 1** (closed-shell RKS). Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz` (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz, 9 atoms: O, 4×C, 4×H); the DFT was a single point on that supplied geometry, not re-optimized. SCF converged in 9 cycles.

**Headline result — HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV** (Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an excitation energy).

Frontier orbital energies:
- HOMO (index 17, occ 2.0) = −6.3597638 eV
- LUMO (index 18, occ 0.0) = −0.1024036 eV
- HOMO−1 = −7.8707441 eV; HOMO−2 = −10.332024 eV
- LUMO+1 = +1.4483308 eV; LUMO+2 = +1.702378 eV

Koopmans-derived quantities: vertical IP = 6.3597638 eV, vertical EA = 0.1024036 eV, electronegativity = 3.2310837 eV, chemical hardness = 3.1286801 eV, chemical softness = 0.3196236 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index = 1.6684195 eV.

Electronic (total) energy: −230.11429 hartree (= −6261.7289 eV = −144398.9 kcal/mol), zero = electronic energy (bare nuclei + electrons). Note that a DFT total-energy origin is method-specific and only directly comparable to same-method, same-basis energies.

**Method used:** DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.

**Convergence/warnings:** SCF converged (scf_converged = true, 9 cycles). The result JSON contained no `warnings` array — no engine warnings were reported. Integrity: status "ok", **trustworthy = true**; all checks passed (energy_finite ok, scf_converged ok, schema_header ok, schema_headline ok).

**Reproducibility:** command — `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpa4366p0j/live.json`. Result JSON written to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpa4366p0j/live.json`. The skill also streams a live `<subcommand>_<timestamp>.out` log to the run directory that can be `tail -f`'d while running.

No experimental/literature comparison is offered — ask if you want one and I'll verify a source first.
